metadata.title: Structural and functional neuroplasticity in music and dance-based rehabilitation: a systematic review
metadata.note: This is a systematic review of 20 RCTs (718 subjects total) examining neuroimaging-based structural and functional neuroplasticity following music- and dance-based interventions in neurological disorders. Claims extracted represent synthesis of findings across multiple studies rather than original experimental results.

claims.claim_text: Dance-based interventions counteract hippocampal atrophy and promote structural neuroplasticity in people at risk of dementia, with increases in hippocampal volume and cortical thickness

axis_proposal.definition: Change in hippocampal volume (mm³) following dance-based intervention relative to control
axis_proposal.psychoacoustic_basis: Structural MRI-derived volumetric measure of hippocampus, critical for memory consolidation

statistics.note: Systematic review; two studies observed positive impact of dance-based intervention on hippocampal volume (Blumen et al., 2023; Zhu et al., 2022)

brain_regions.context: Dance-based interventions showed increases in right and total hippocampal volume in people at risk of dementia
Increased cortical thickness in fusiform and occipital cortex following dance intervention
Increased cortical thickness in occipital cortex

methodology.task: Dance-based rehabilitation programs (ballroom dancing, aerobic dance, choreography learning)
methodology.control_conditions: Treadmill walking, health education, life as usual, standard care
methodology.sample_size: 271 subjects at risk of dementia across multiple studies
methodology.participant_characteristics: Adults ≥18 years with MCI or subjective memory complaints

claims.claim_text: Music-based interventions promote structural neuroplasticity in language-related brain regions in stroke patients, with increases in grey matter volume in left and right frontal areas, anterior cingulate cortex, and ventral striatum

dependent_variable.name: Grey matter volume in language regions

axis_proposal.definition: Change in grey matter volume (mm³) in language-related regions (frontal, temporal, insula) following music intervention
axis_proposal.psychoacoustic_basis: VBM-derived volumetric measure of language network regions

statistics.note: Systematic review; five studies showed significant impact on structural neuroplasticity related to language recovery (Sihvonen et al., 2020, 2021, 2022; Särkämö et al., 2014)

brain_regions.context: Increased GMV in left STG, middle MTG, and inferior ITG following vocal music listening
Increased GMV in left MTG
Increased GMV in right MTG and MOG following instrumental music listening
Increased GMV in left insula in patients with right hemisphere lesions, correlated with language improvement
Increased GMV in limbic regions including anterior cingulate, associated with mood changes
Increased GMV in right ventral striatum

methodology.task: Passive music listening (vocal or instrumental), home-based music listening
methodology.sample_size: 197 patients with acute stroke across multiple studies
methodology.participant_characteristics: Adults with acute stroke, left or right hemisphere lesions

claims.claim_text: Music-based interventions enhance white matter integrity in language pathways (FAT, AF, SLF, corpus callosum, CST) in stroke patients, with increases in fractional anisotropy and quantitative anisotropy

axis_proposal.definition: Change in fractional anisotropy (FA) or quantitative anisotropy (QA) in white matter tracts following music intervention
axis_proposal.psychoacoustic_basis: DWI-derived measure of white matter tract integrity, indicating axonal organization and myelination

statistics.note: Systematic review; multiple studies showed increases in FA and QA in language pathways (Sihvonen, Ripollés et al., 2021; Sihvonen et al., 2022; Sihvonen et al., 2024)

brain_regions.context: Increased FA in left FAT following vocal music listening, correlated with improved language skills
Increased QA in left and right AF following music interventions
Increased QA in left and right SLF
Increased QA in corpus callosum
Increased QA in CST

methodology.participant_characteristics: Adults with acute or chronic stroke

claims.claim_text: Music-based interventions enhance functional connectivity within language networks and between language network and default mode network in stroke patients

axis_proposal.definition: Change in functional connectivity strength (correlation coefficient) within language network and between language network and DMN following music intervention
axis_proposal.psychoacoustic_basis: rs-fMRI or task-fMRI derived measure of synchronized activity between brain regions

statistics.note: Systematic review; Sihvonen et al. (2020) showed increased FC between left STG/MTG and DMN; Sihvonen, Pitkäniemi et al. (2021) showed increased FC within language network

brain_regions.context: Increased FC between left STG/MTG and DMN following vocal music listening
Increased FC between left STG/MTG and DMN
Increased FC between left inferior parietal/postcentral areas and language network
Increased FC between left supramarginal gyrus and language network following instrumental music listening
Increased FC between language regions and DMN

methodology.task: Music listening interventions, passive listening tasks during fMRI
methodology.participant_characteristics: Adults with acute or chronic stroke

claims.claim_text: Active music playing (Neurological Music Therapy) promotes executive function recovery and structural neuroplasticity in right frontal areas, left cerebellum, and fusiform gyrus in traumatic brain injury patients

dependent_variable.name: Grey matter volume in frontal and cerebellar regions

axis_proposal.definition: Change in grey matter volume (mm³) in frontal, cerebellar, and fusiform regions following active music playing
axis_proposal.psychoacoustic_basis: VBM-derived volumetric measure of motor and cognitive regions

statistics.note: Systematic review; Siponkoski et al. (2020) showed increased GMV in right IFG, right MFG, left cerebellum, and left fusiform gyrus

brain_regions.context: Increased GMV in right IFG, correlated with Number Letter Task performance
Increased GMV in right MFG
Increased GMV in left cerebellum
Increased GMV in left fusiform gyrus

methodology.task: Neurological Music Therapy with active musical production (drums or piano)

claims.claim_text: Dance-based interventions improve microstructural integrity of white matter bundles (superior longitudinal fasciculus, corpus callosum, corticospinal tract) essential for sensorimotor integration and interhemispheric communication

axis_proposal.definition: Change in white matter integrity (FA, MD, RD) in sensorimotor and interhemispheric tracts following dance intervention
axis_proposal.psychoacoustic_basis: DWI-derived measure of white matter tract integrity

statistics.note: Systematic review; Rektorova et al. (2019) found increased RD bilaterally in SLF, left CC, and left CST; increased MD bilaterally in SLF, left CC, and bilateral CST

brain_regions.context: Increased RD and MD bilaterally in SLF following dance intervention
Increased RD and MD in left corpus callosum
Increased RD and MD bilaterally in CST

methodology.sample_size: Multiple studies with people at risk of dementia
methodology.participant_characteristics: Adults with MCI or risk of dementia

c3_associations.process: Dance-based interventions promote hippocampal neuroplasticity and memory consolidation in people at risk of dementia
c3_associations.evidence: Dance-based interventions counteract hippocampal atrophy and promote increases in hippocampal volume, associated with improvements in memory abilities. Increased ALFF in entorhinal and para-hippocampal cortex following dance intervention.

c3_associations.process: Music-based interventions promote structural and functional neuroplasticity in auditory and language processing regions in stroke patients
c3_associations.evidence: Music-based interventions show increases in GMV in left STG, MTG, and increased functional connectivity within language network and between language network and DMN. Enhanced white matter integrity in language pathways (AF, FAT, SLF).

c3_associations.process: Active music playing promotes motor and executive function recovery through structural neuroplasticity in frontal and cerebellar regions
c3_associations.evidence: Active music playing (Neurological Music Therapy) with drums or piano promotes increases in GMV in right IFG, right MFG, and left cerebellum, associated with improvements in executive function and motor skills in TBI patients.

c3_associations.process: Dance-based interventions enhance sensorimotor integration through improved white matter integrity in sensorimotor and interhemispheric tracts
c3_associations.evidence: Dance-based interventions improve microstructural integrity of white matter bundles (SLF, corpus callosum, CST) essential for sensorimotor integration, interhemispheric communication, and motor control.

c3_associations.process: Music-based interventions enhance functional connectivity within language networks and between language network and default mode network, supporting predictive coding mechanisms

brain_regions.role: Predictive coding and internal model generation

c3_associations.evidence: Music-based interventions show increased functional connectivity between language regions (STG, MTG) and DMN, and within language network, supporting predictive coding mechanisms for language recovery.

c3_associations.process: Music-based interventions promote mood regulation through structural neuroplasticity in limbic regions including anterior cingulate cortex and ventral striatum
c3_associations.evidence: Music-based interventions show increases in GMV in limbic regions including anterior cingulate cortex and ventral striatum, associated with improvements in mood (decreased depression, tension, fatigue, irritability).

r3_coordinates.note: This is a systematic review; no specific experimental variables mapped to R³ coordinates. However, the paper discusses interventions involving music (pitch, timbre, tempo, rhythm) and dance (tempo, rhythm, beat) that could be mapped to L4, L5, and L6 dimensions.

mentions.context: Dance-based interventions counteract hippocampal atrophy and promote increases in hippocampal volume
Increased GMV in left STG following vocal music listening
Increased FC between left STG/MTG and DMN
Increased FA in left FAT following vocal music listening, correlated with improved language skills
Increased QA in left and right AF following music interventions
Increased GMV in right IFG, correlated with Number Letter Task performance
Increased FC between language regions and DMN
Predictive coding and internal model generation
Increased GMV in anterior cingulate, associated with mood changes
Increased QA in left and right SLF
Increased RD and MD bilaterally in SLF following dance intervention

brain_regions_summary.note: This is a systematic review of 20 RCTs; all brain region mentions are from literature inference based on reviewed studies. No original experimental data reported. Total of 25 unique brain regions mentioned across claims and C³ associations.

warnings: All DV r3_axis unmapped (axis_proposal provided for all)
Systematic review paper with no original experimental data (scope: literature_inference)
No specific R³ coordinates mapped (discusses interventions involving music/dance but no experimental variables)

data_quality.note: Systematic review of 20 RCTs (718 subjects total); no original statistics or experimental data. All brain regions collected with full context from literature synthesis. Comprehensive coverage of structural and functional neuroplasticity findings.
